title: SKU to Feature Mapping and Upgrade Path Design
description:
Spike to design the SKU-to-feature mapping and upgrade path system for DataTools Pro licensing tiers. The goal is to enable feature gating based on license level, prompting free users to upgrade when they encounter premium features.

Licensing tiers:
- **Contributor** (free) — single user with feature restrictions
- **Team** ($9.99) — available for online self-service purchase, adds user seats
- **Enterprise** — directly managed in Salesforce, not available online
- **Integrator** — different license agreement and pricing, managed in Salesforce

The design includes a middleware that checks the account's available seats to determine premium feature access. A WordPress custom post type ('Upgrade Windows') serves as a CMS for marketing-controlled upgrade modal content, loaded dynamically into the DataTools app. Each upgrade prompt is associated with a slug for the triggering feature.

Future plans include 1:1 SKU-to-feature mapping allowing granular feature enablement per plan, and Stripe integration via Laravel Cashier for subscription management.
acceptance_criteria: Middleware checks account seats to determine premium feature access
Free users are prompted to upgrade when accessing premium features
Licensing tiers (Contributor, Team, Enterprise, Integrator) are defined in the data model
Admin UI exists for managing plans with CRUD operations
WordPress custom post type for upgrade modal content is created
Upgrade modal content loads dynamically from WordPress REST API
ux_changes: Upgrade prompts appear when free users access premium features. Modal content is dynamically loaded from WordPress CMS.
middleware_changes: New middleware checks account seat count to gate premium features. Admin-only plan management screens created.
data_model_changes: Plans model enhanced with SKU fields, licensing tier types. Future: 1:1 mapping between SKUs and features. WordPress custom post type 'Upgrade Windows' for CMS-managed content.
api_changes: WordPress REST API serves upgrade modal content. Future: Stripe integration for subscription billing.
implementation_notes: Each tenant gets one license tier applied to all users. Billing is restricted to the account owner. Feature gating checks available seats as a proxy for paid status. Stripe integration is planned via Laravel Cashier but not yet implemented.
comment_summary: Extensive design discussion about licensing tiers, feature gating, and upgrade paths. Product clarified Salesforce-style licensing (per-tenant, not per-feature). Developer built admin plan management UI and middleware for feature gating. WordPress custom post type created for marketing-controlled upgrade content.
